servers.name: playwright

permissions.explanation: Playwright MCP gives the model browser automation capabilities such as navigation, clicking, typing, form filling, screenshots, and tab interactions.
permissions.recommendation: Use an isolated browser profile and avoid sensitive logged-in sessions.

permissions.evidence: Playwright browser automation can navigate to URLs and make browser network requests.
permissions.explanation: The server can access network resources through browser navigation and page interactions.
permissions.recommendation: Use --allowed-origins or --blocked-origins to constrain browser network access.

permissions.explanation: Playwright MCP documents an unsafe tool (browser_run_code_unsafe) that can run Playwright code in the server process. This is a well-known, Microsoft-maintained package; the tool exists for advanced use cases but should be treated as RCE-equivalent unless disabled by policy or client-side tool allowlisting.
permissions.recommendation: Require explicit approval for this tool, disable it via client tool allowlists, or avoid enabling it in sensitive environments.

permissions.evidence: No --isolated option found in Playwright MCP configuration.
permissions.explanation: The browser profile may persist cookies, local storage, and login state between sessions unless isolated mode is used.
permissions.recommendation: Add --isolated unless persistent login state is intentionally required.

permissions.evidence: Playwright MCP supports browser file upload/drop style interactions.
permissions.explanation: Some browser file interactions can reference local file paths. This is not the same as unrestricted filesystem access.
permissions.recommendation: Avoid exposing sensitive files through upload/drop tools and review any configured file access flags.

installRisks.explanation: The npx package uses @latest, which can change over time.
installRisks.recommendation: Pin the package to a specific version, for example @playwright/mcp@<version>.

recommendations: Use an isolated browser profile and avoid sensitive logged-in sessions.
Use --allowed-origins or --blocked-origins to constrain browser network access.
Require explicit approval for this tool, disable it via client tool allowlists, or avoid enabling it in sensitive environments.
Add --isolated unless persistent login state is intentionally required.
Avoid exposing sensitive files through upload/drop tools and review any configured file access flags.
Pin the package to a specific version, for example @playwright/mcp@<version>.

saferConfigSuggestions.title: Pin package version
saferConfigSuggestions.explanation: Pin the npx package to a specific version for reproducible installs.

saferConfigSuggestions.title: Use isolated browser mode
saferConfigSuggestions.explanation: Add --isolated to prevent browser state (cookies, login sessions, local storage) from persisting between runs.

saferConfigSuggestions.title: Restrict browser origins
saferConfigSuggestions.explanation: Configure --allowed-origins or --blocked-origins to limit which sites the browser can navigate to.

adjustments.reason: Known server profile matched: Microsoft Playwright MCP (Microsoft)
adjustments.evidence: Official Microsoft Playwright MCP package/repository match.

scoringTrace.explanation: Capability impact is **critical** because the server has critical-impact capabilities (browser.control, network.fetch, code.execution). Publisher trust is **high** because the package matches a known Microsoft profile. Install hygiene is **C** because the npx package uses @latest, which can change over time.. Config hardening is **C**. Effective risk is **high**.
